label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to figure this out. So the question is, if I follow these steps, do I end up back where I started? The options are yes or no. Let me break down the instructions step by step. First, take 3 steps. Now, when they say "take 3 steps," I assume that's in a straight line. Let's say I start facing north. So, I go 3 steps north. Then, turn around. Turning around would mean 180 degrees, so now I'm facing south. So, if I was facing north before, now I'm facing south. Then take 5 steps. So, 5 steps south. So from my original position, I went 3 steps north, then 5 steps south. That would be a net movement of 5 - 3 = 2 steps south from the starting point. Then, turn right. If I'm facing south, a right turn would face me west. Wait, no. Let me think. If you're facing south and turn right, your right would be west?
